result.markdown:
--- title: "Phase 2 — Noncompartmental Analysis (NCA)" subtitle: "Per-subject exposure metrics with PKNCA" author: "Pramod BR" date: today format: html: theme: cosmo toc: true toc-depth: 3 code-fold: true code-tools: true number-sections: true fig-width: 8 fig-height: 6 execute: echo: true warning: false message: false --- ## Objectives Noncompartmental analysis (NCA) summarises drug exposure **without assuming a structural model**. It provides model-independent benchmarks — peak concentration, exposure (AUC), and terminal half-life — that we will later compare against the population-model estimates (Phases 3–5). Parameters computed per subject: - **Cmax / Tmax** — observed peak concentration and its time. - **AUClast** — area under the curve to the last measurable concentration (linear-up/log-down trapezoidal). - **AUCinf(obs)** — AUClast plus the extrapolated tail (Clast / λz). - **t½** — terminal half-life, from the terminal rate constant **λz**. - **CL/F, Vz/F** — apparent clearance and volume (dose-normalised). ## Method (PKNCA) `run_nca()` builds a `PKNCAconc` object (concentration–time, grouped by subject), a `PKNCAdose` object (the single oral dose in mg at t = 0), defines one **0 → ∞** interval requesting the parameters above, and runs `pk.nca()`. PKNCA selects the terminal-phase points for λz automatically by maximising the adjusted R². ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} df <- load_pk_data() nca <- run_nca(df) ``` ::: ## Per-subject NCA parameters ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} nca$wide |> dplyr::select(id, cmax, tmax, auclast, aucinf.obs, half.life, cl.obs, vz.obs, aucpext.obs) |> kbl( caption = "Noncompartmental parameters by subject", digits = 2, col.names = c("ID", "Cmax (mg/L)", "Tmax (h)", "AUClast (mg·h/L)", "AUCinf (mg·h/L)", "t½ (h)", "CL/F (L/h)", "Vz/F (L)", "AUC %extrap") ) |> kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Noncompartmental parameters by subject
ID Cmax (mg/L) Tmax (h) AUClast (mg·h/L) AUCinf (mg·h/L) t½ (h) CL/F (L/h) Vz/F (L) AUC %extrap
1 10.50 1.12 147.23 214.92 14.30 1.49 30.73 31.49
2 8.33 1.92 88.73 97.38 6.66 3.27 31.43 8.88
3 8.20 1.02 95.88 106.13 6.77 3.01 29.37 9.66
4 8.60 1.07 102.63 114.22 6.98 2.80 28.21 10.14
5 11.40 1.00 118.18 136.30 8.00 2.35 27.10 13.30
6 6.44 1.15 71.70 82.18 7.89 3.89 44.35 12.75
7 7.09 3.48 87.97 100.99 7.85 3.17 35.85 12.89
8 7.56 2.02 86.81 102.15 8.51 3.13 38.38 15.02
9 9.03 0.63 83.94 97.52 8.41 2.75 33.31 13.93
10 10.21 3.55 135.58 167.86 9.25 1.91 25.44 19.23
11 8.00 0.98 77.89 86.90 7.26 3.68 38.55 10.37
12 9.75 3.52 115.22 125.83 6.29 2.55 23.11 8.43
````` ::: ::: The **AUC %extrapolated** column is a quality flag — values well under 20% indicate AUCinf is reliable (little of the exposure comes from extrapolation). ## Summary statistics `nca_param_summary()` reports the **geometric mean** and **geometric CV%** — the conventional summaries for PK parameters, which are log-normally distributed. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} smry <- nca_param_summary(nca$wide) smry |> kbl(caption = "NCA parameter summary (geometric mean, CV%, range)", digits = 2) |> kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
NCA parameter summary (geometric mean, CV%, range)
Parameter N Geo. mean Geo. CV% Min Median Max
cmax 12 8.65 16.98 6.44 8.46 11.40
tmax 12 1.52 64.67 0.63 1.14 3.55
auclast 12 98.65 22.54 71.70 92.30 147.23
aucinf.obs 12 114.81 28.43 82.18 104.14 214.92
half.life 12 7.99 21.85 6.29 7.87 14.30
cl.obs 12 2.74 27.86 1.49 2.90 3.89
vz.obs 12 31.62 19.21 23.11 31.08 44.35
aucpext.obs 12 12.89 38.17 8.43 12.82 31.49
````` ::: ::: For theophylline, the literature half-life in healthy adults is roughly **6–9 h**; this NCA gives a geometric-mean **t½ ≈ 8 h**, apparent clearance **CL/F ≈ 2.74 L/h**, and apparent volume **Vz/F ≈ 31.6 L** — all physiologically sensible and a useful sanity check before population modelling. ## Visualisations ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} nca_plot_halflife(nca$wide) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Terminal half-life by subject (dashed line = geometric mean).](02_NCA_Report_files/figure-html/fig-halflife-1.png){#fig-halflife width=100%} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} nca_plot_auc(nca$wide) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![AUClast vs AUCinf — proximity to the identity line indicates minimal extrapolation.](02_NCA_Report_files/figure-html/fig-auc-1.png){#fig-auc width=100%} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} nca_plot_distributions(nca$wide) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distribution of NCA parameters across the 12 subjects.](02_NCA_Report_files/figure-html/fig-distributions-1.png){#fig-distributions width=100%} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} nca_plot_cl_weight(nca$wide, df) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Apparent clearance vs body weight — an exploratory look ahead to the covariate model.](02_NCA_Report_files/figure-html/fig-cl-weight-1.png){#fig-cl-weight width=100%} ::: ::: The Pearson correlation between weight and NCA clearance is **r = 0.17** (p = 0.59) — suggestive, and formally tested inside the population model in Phase 5. ## Key takeaways - NCA gives **model-independent** exposure metrics that frame the modelling work. - Geometric-mean **t½ ≈ 8 h**, **CL/F ≈ 2.74 L/h**, **Vz/F ≈ 31.6 L** are consistent with known theophylline PK. - Low AUC %extrapolation means AUCinf (and therefore CL/Vz) is well determined. - Between-subject spread in CL and AUC reinforces the need for an **IIV model** (Phase 4) and motivates testing **weight** as a covariate (Phase 5). > Next: **Phase 3 — Base PopPK model** (one-compartment oral, first-order > absorption) with `nlmixr2` / `rxode2`.
